content.problemStatement:
You are given a string SSS consisting of lowercase English letters.

Count the number of ways to break the string SSS into 333 strings P,Q,P, Q,P,Q, and RRR such that:


Concatenating P,Q,P, Q,P,Q, and RRR in order produces SSS, i.e., P+Q+R=SP + Q + R = SP+Q+R=S;
Concatenating PPP and RRR in order produces QQQ, i.e., P+R=QP + R = QP+R=Q.
Note that P,Q,RP, Q, RP,Q,R may be empty strings.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case contains a string SSS consisting of lowercase English letters.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, a single integer denoting the number of ways to break the string such that the given conditions are met.
constraints: The sum of ∣S∣|S|∣S∣ over all test cases does not exceed 3⋅1053 \cdot 10^53⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 1: Consider P=aP = \text{a}P=a, Q=abQ = \text{ab}Q=ab, R=bR = \text{b}R=b. Thus,

P+Q+R=a+ab+b=aabb=SP + Q + R = \text{a} + \text{ab} + \text{b} = \text{aabb} = SP+Q+R=a+ab+b=aabb=S;
P+R=a+b=ab=QP + R = \text{a} + \text{b} = \text{ab} = QP+R=a+b=ab=Q.

It can be shown that this is the only way to break the string such that the conditions are met.
Test case 2: The 444 triplets of P,Q,RP, Q, RP,Q,R which satisfy the conditions: (,aaa,aaa),(a,aaa,aa),(aa,aaa,a),(aaa,aaa,)( \text{} , \text{aaa}, \text{aaa}), (\text{a}, \text{aaa}, \text{aa}), (\text{aa}, \text{aaa}, \text{a}), (\text{aaa}, \text{aaa}, \text{})(,aaa,aaa),(a,aaa,aa),(aa,aaa,a),(aaa,aaa,).
Note that PPP is an empty string in (,aaa,aaa)(\text{}, \text{aaa}, \text{aaa})(,aaa,aaa) and RRR is an empty string in (aaa,aaa,)(\text{aaa}, \text{aaa}, \text{})(aaa,aaa,).
